title: Scroll reveal hard-fails when IntersectionObserver is unavailable
reasoning: The reveal script is loaded globally and only has a reduced-motion branch before constructing IntersectionObserver. In browsers, embedded webviews, or test environments without IntersectionObserver, initScrollReveal throws instead of gracefully marking reveal elements visible, so progressive enhancement can become a content/animation failure.
reproduction: Run the page in an environment where window.IntersectionObserver is undefined and dispatch astro:page-load with at least one .reveal element present; the handler throws before adding visible or observing elements.
recommendation: Feature-detect IntersectionObserver before constructing it. If unavailable, add visible to all reveal elements and return, matching the reduced-motion graceful-degradation behavior. Consider also guarding matchMedia for older/non-browser-like environments.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed for this feature, and the script has no exercised fallback path for missing browser APIs.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a DOM test that creates a .reveal element, temporarily removes window.IntersectionObserver, dispatches astro:page-load, and asserts the element receives visible without throwing.
minimumFixScope: Update src/scripts/scroll-reveal.js to add browser API fallback handling around matchMedia and IntersectionObserver.

history.reasoning: The original path still exists. The old evidence ranges have moved/changed: src/scripts/scroll-reveal.js now guards matchMedia via prefersReducedMotion() at lines 20-23 and explicitly falls back when typeof window.IntersectionObserver !== 'function' at lines 33-36, calling revealAll() and returning before constructing IntersectionObserver. A targeted Node DOM-stub reproduction with window.IntersectionObserver absent and a .reveal element present dispatched astro:page-load successfully and marked the element visible without throwing. There is still no committed scroll-reveal regression test found under tests/src, and the full Vitest suite could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vitest/Vite attempted to create cache/temp files and failed with EPERM, but the current code and targeted reproduction are sufficient to mark this specific finding fixed.
commands: node --input-type=module -e "const listeners = new Map(); const reveal = { added: [], classList: { add(value) { reveal.added.push(value); } } }; globalThis.window = {}; globalThis.document = { addEventListener(type, handler) { listeners.set(type, handler); }, querySelectorAll(selector) { if (selector !== '.reveal') throw new Error('unexpected selector ' + selector); return [reveal]; } }; await import('./src/scripts/scroll-reveal.js?missing-io-revalidation'); const handler = listeners.get('astro:page-load'); if (typeof handler !== 'function') throw new Error('astro:page-load handler was not registered'); handler(); if (!reveal.added.includes('visible')) throw new Error('reveal element was not marked visible'); console.log('missing IntersectionObserver fallback passed');"
